checkedCadence: Weekly source check; urgent changes can be added through the Signal Intel Desk daily task.
method: Sourced, dated estimates only. No fake live counters. Red counters show figures or estimate markers only; titles and explanations render underneath. Where a reliable single estimate is not available, the counter uses an estimate-status value such as EST. SOURCE-SPLIT or EST. UNDERCOUNT. Crime, sexual assault, trafficking, migrant-status, nationality, ethnicity, suspect, charge, conviction, victim, and report categories must not be merged. This file was checked on 2026-06-29 against official or high-reliability lanes; unchanged live figures mean no stronger sourced replacement was found during the check.

panels.title: Gaza / Israel War
panels.status: Gaza Health Ministry reported death toll lane; contested combatant/civilian split
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. AP reported the Gaza Health Ministry figure had passed 73,050 deaths since 2023-10-07, with women and children around half of deaths, and noted the ministry does not separate combatants from civilians. Keep Gaza deaths, Israeli deaths, hostages, injuries, child deaths, famine indicators, disputed official claims, and ceasefire-period casualties separate.
panels.sourceLabel: AP citing Gaza Health Ministry / OCHA OPT / OHCHR / UN Commission

panels.title: Ukraine War
panels.status: OHCHR verified civilian baseline; real toll likely higher
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. OHCHR-linked reporting and AP summaries still support a conservative verified civilian death baseline above 15,000, while recent reporting says May 2026 was the highest monthly civilian casualty month since April 2022. Keep civilian deaths, injuries, military losses, missing persons, displacement, occupied-territory reporting limits, and infrastructure attacks separate.
panels.sourceLabel: OHCHR Ukraine / AP / Reuters / UNHCR / OCHA

panels.title: Sudan / Darfur
panels.status: High-uncertainty conflict estimate; displacement and famine tracked separately
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. No stronger official total death count replaced the high-uncertainty 150,000+ estimate lane. UN-linked summaries continue to show nearly 12 million displaced, severe famine and food-insecurity conditions, major health-system collapse, and large reporting gaps. Keep verified killings, mass-casualty reports, displacement, famine, hospital attacks, siege conditions, cholera, deportations, and humanitarian access collapse separate.
panels.sourceLabel: OCHA / OHCHR Sudan / UNHCR / IOM / WFP / WHO / Reuters

panels.title: Myanmar Conflict
panels.status: High-uncertainty conflict estimate; source-check retained
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. No cleaner official nationwide death total was found during this run. Current reporting shows continuing civilian harm, including escalating junta aerial attacks. Track civilian deaths, airstrikes, displacement, political detention, ethnic conflict, aid restriction, hospital/school attacks, and humanitarian access separately.
panels.sourceLabel: UN OHCHR / OCHA / UNHCR / major wire or rights reporting where primary data is unavailable

panels.title: Yemen War / Humanitarian Collapse
panels.status: Conflict and indirect mortality estimate; not a live counter
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. The 377,000+ UNDP conflict-and-indirect-mortality estimate remains the latest stable long-war baseline in this lane. Track direct conflict deaths, indirect excess mortality, hunger, cholera/disease, displacement, port access, blockade claims, migrant-route deaths, and humanitarian operations separately.
panels.sourceLabel: UNDP / OCHA Yemen / WHO / WFP / IPC / IOM

panels.title: DR Congo / Great Lakes
panels.status: Long-war mortality estimate lane; current crisis indicators separate
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. The long-war 6M+ mortality lane remains an historical estimate and must not be treated as a daily counter. Track current armed-group violence, civilian killings, sexual violence reports, displacement, disease outbreaks, mineral/cross-border pressure, and MONUSCO/OCHA updates separately.
panels.sourceLabel: OCHA DRC / UNHCR / MONUSCO / WHO / historical mortality studies

panels.title: Syria War Aftermath
panels.status: Long-war mortality estimate lane; period and source split required
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. No single official total replaced the long-war estimate lane. Track deaths, detention, disappeared persons, displacement, returns, refugee pressure, sanctions, infrastructure collapse, and humanitarian access as separate evidence categories.
panels.sourceLabel: OCHA Syria / UNHCR / OHCHR / Syrian casualty monitors

panels.title: Global Displacement
panels.status: UNHCR Global Trends baseline for end-2024 / 2025 release
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. UNHCR's latest Global Trends lane reported more than 122 million people forcibly displaced worldwide, including 73.5 million internally displaced people; AP's UNHCR summary put the figure above 122 million and highlighted Sudan as the largest displacement crisis. Keep refugees, IDPs, asylum seekers, stateless people, returns, and temporary protection separate.
panels.sourceLabel: UNHCR Global Trends / AP summary

panels.title: Famine / Acute Food Insecurity
panels.status: GRFC 2025 high acute food insecurity baseline
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. The Global Report on Food Crises lane reported 295.3 million people facing high acute food insecurity across 53 analysed countries, with catastrophe-level conditions concentrated heavily in Gaza and Sudan. IPC phases, famine declarations, famine risk, siege conditions, nutrition collapse, funding cuts, and humanitarian access restrictions must be tracked separately.
panels.sourceLabel: Global Report on Food Crises / IPC / FAO / WFP / Guardian summary

panels.title: Migration / Irregular Immigration
panels.status: Country and route data only; no honest global illegal-immigration counter
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. No reliable single global irregular-migration counter exists. Use route/country panels: EU irregular border detections were just over 239,000 in 2024; EU first-time asylum applications were about 912,000 in 2024; the U.S. unauthorized population was estimated at a record 14 million in 2023; and UK small-boat detections passed 200,000 cumulatively since 2018 by May 2026. Keep detections, persons, repeat crossings, asylum applications, returns, removals, departures, estimated unauthorized populations, and missing migrants separate.
panels.sourceLabel: Frontex / Eurostat / CBP / UK Home Office / Pew / AP / Reuters

panels.title: Migration Deaths / Missing Migrants
panels.status: IOM 2025 global deaths/missing lane; undercount warning
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. Reuters reported IOM's 2025 global total as nearly 8,000 migrants dead or missing, down from 2024 but likely undercounted due to reduced monitoring. Route examples included Mediterranean, Atlantic/Canary, Asian and Horn of Africa routes. Keep global totals, route totals, confirmed deaths, missing persons, and unverified/undocumented cases separate.
panels.sourceLabel: IOM Missing Migrants / Reuters

panels.title: UK Child Sexual Abuse & Exploitation
panels.status: 2024 police-recorded CSAE lane retained
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. No newer official national replacement was found in this run. England/Wales police recorded 122,768 child sexual abuse and exploitation offences in 2024, with online CSAE reported up 26%. Track offence reports, contact offences, online offences, group-based offences, grooming-gang subset, suspects, charges, convictions, victim age, and data completeness separately.
panels.sourceLabel: NPCC / official police-data reporting / UK media summaries

panels.title: Group-Based CSE / Grooming-Gang Data
panels.status: 2024 group-based CSE lane retained with data-gap warning
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. The 2024 lane remains: 4,450 group-based child sexual exploitation offences across 44 police forces, with 761 linked to grooming gangs. Ethnicity data was recorded in only about one-third of cases, so the site must show both the numbers and the data gap. Keep suspects, convictions, victims, force coverage, and unknown-status categories separate.
panels.sourceLabel: NPCC-reported data / Casey audit lane / AP / UK media

panels.title: Sexual Assault / Migrant-Status Evidence Boundary
panels.status: Country-by-country crime-data lane; no merged group-guilt counter
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. Use this only where official statistics identify suspect nationality, immigration status, asylum status, foreign-born status, or ethnicity. Separate police reports, suspects, charges, convictions, victims, unknown status, and per-capita rates. Do not treat nationality as immigration status, and do not treat individual cases as group guilt.
panels.sourceLabel: National crime offices / courts / police datasets / Eurostat where applicable

panels.title: Trafficking / Sexual Exploitation
panels.status: UNODC 2024 detected-victims lane; child-share included
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. Reuters' summary of UNODC's 2024 Global Report reported 69,627 detected trafficking victims in 2022 and children at 38% of detected victims, with sexual exploitation a major category, especially for women and girls. Keep detected victims, estimated victims, exploitation type, forced labour, sexual exploitation, child share, convictions, and profit estimates separate.
panels.sourceLabel: UNODC Global Report 2024 / Reuters / ILO forced-labour profits

panels.title: Vaccines: Damages & Compensation
panels.status: Compensation and application lane; not raw adverse-event lane
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. The U.S. VICP historical compensation baseline remains above $4.6B. UK Covid Inquiry reporting cited 17,519 COVID-vaccine-related VDPS applications and recommended reform of the payment level. Adverse-event reports, compensated claims, pending claims, rejected claims, causal findings, litigation claims, and scheme applications must be separated.
panels.sourceLabel: HRSA VICP / CICP / UK Covid Inquiry / UK VDPS / FT summary

panels.title: Children / Trafficking / Missing
panels.status: NCMEC 2024 CyberTipline baseline; reports not victims
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. NCMEC CyberTipline data for 2024 showed 20.5 million reports of suspected child sexual exploitation, representing 29.2 million incidents and nearly 63 million files. This is a reporting volume, not a count of unique victims or missing children. Missing-child cases, online exploitation reports, trafficking indicators, and resolved cases must not be merged.
panels.sourceLabel: NCMEC CyberTipline Data / ICMEC / UNODC / Europol / Interpol

panels.title: Missing Children Cases Supported
panels.status: NCMEC support-case baseline retained
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. NCMEC-supported missing-child cases remain a support-case figure, not total missing children worldwide, trafficking victims, or CyberTipline volume. Keep family abduction, endangered runaway, lost/injured/missing, non-family abduction, recovery, and case-support categories separate.
panels.sourceLabel: NCMEC impact data

panels.title: Online Enticement / Sextortion Reports
panels.status: NCMEC 2024 online solicitation/enticement reporting lane
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. Guardian reporting on NCMEC data cited 546,000 reports from tech firms in 2024 involving adults soliciting children, up 192% from 2023, while NCMEC-related reporting also notes AI-related reports increased sharply. Treat this as suspected-report volume, not proven offender or victim count. Keep online enticement, financial sextortion, AI-generated reports, platform-reporting changes, and law-enforcement outcomes separate.
panels.sourceLabel: NCMEC / FBI / NCA / Guardian / Axios / Wired

panels.title: Medical / System Failure
panels.status: Healthcare pressure lane; theater-by-theater only
panels.description: Checked 2026-06-29. No single honest global medical-collapse counter exists. Use country/theater panels: Sudan reporting indicates most health facilities disrupted and thousands killed in healthcare-related attacks; Gaza, Ukraine, Yemen, DRC, and Myanmar require separate lanes for hospital attacks, medicine shortages, blocked aid, disease outbreaks, healthcare-worker deaths, excess mortality, and service collapse.
panels.sourceLabel: WHO / OCHA / national health authorities / court records / Reuters / AP
